critical.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — webhook triggers builds AND state updates flow back to Linear
critical.reality: webhook.py contains only inbound parsing (verify_linear_signature, parse_linear_event, linear_issue_to_brief) — no outbound Linear API calls exist in any provided file. There is no issueUpdate GraphQL mutation, no linear_update_issue helper, and no HTTP call to api.linear.app that would push state changes (e.g. In Progress on build start, Done on build complete) back to Linear.
critical.recommendation: Add an async helper (e.g. async def linear_set_issue_state(issue_id, state_id)) that POSTs the issueUpdate mutation to https://api.linear.app/graphql with LINEAR_API_KEY auth, and call it from routes/webhooks.py at build-start and build-complete events.

high.claim: spec claims Linear two-way sync — state updates flow back to Linear (implying issue lifecycle is managed: Todo → In Progress → Done)
high.reality: parse_linear_event only reads inbound state transitions (action=update stateId change, action=create for Urgent/High). The outbound half — updating the Linear issue state when the Pi-CEO session transitions (started, completed, failed) — is absent from all provided source files.
high.recommendation: Wire linear_set_issue_state calls into the session lifecycle hooks in routes/sessions.py at the points where session.status transitions to 'running', 'complete', and 'failed'.
